Explore Cumbria

Country-house hospitality, handled properly.

For private travellers, family weeks, and milestone celebrations across the Lake District.

Start a Conversation

The Region

Why Cumbria

Cumbria has more space than most of England. Country houses with enough beds for an extended family under one roof. Lake-front estates with grounds. The county sits between Manchester and Glasgow, which means guests travel in from across the country in a single day.

The valleys vary. Ullswater is wide and quiet on the eastern side. Windermere is busier on the western road and calmer above it. Coniston runs south, narrower, with fewer cars. Each valley has its own character.

Autumn turns the bracken to rust. Winter empties the fells. May brings the rhododendrons at Holker into colour. August is when the Lakes get crowded; we tend to send people in September and again in late spring.

The Lakes are walking country. Most houses on our list sit within a short walk of a fell or a lake. Cumbria stays private.

The region

The journey

Thirteen houses across the region. Click a pin for the notes we keep on each.

Click the pins. Scroll the page, not the map.

Tell us what you need.

The houses

Where we put people in Cumbria

The properties we return to, chosen for the kitchen as often as the view.

Cartmel · Restaurant with rooms

L'Enclume

Simon Rogan's three Michelin-starred and Michelin Green Star flagship inside a thirteenth-century smithy. The rooms are dotted around the charming village of Cartmel. Getting the room is the hard part.

Michelin

High Newton · Village inn

Heft

A Michelin star, no fuss. Kevin Tickle's daily-changing tasting menu in a village inn above the southern Lakes. Regional produce, no ceremony.

Michelin

Windermere · Country house

Storrs Hall

Grade II* Georgian mansion on its own promontory into Windermere. Adults-only, 30 rooms, landscaped gardens running to the lake.

Windermere · Country house

Gilpin Hotel & Lake House

Cedar spa suites, SOURCE holds one Michelin star, family run since 1987. Takes a larger party without losing the feel of the house.

Michelin

Windermere · Country house

Linthwaite House

Directly above the lake. Henrock holds one Michelin star. Simon Rogan. Tarn walks from the door and the water visible from most rooms.

Michelin

Windermere · Country house

Langdale Chase

Grade II listed manor on the eastern shore of Windermere, reopened 2023 after a full restoration. Two AA Rosettes, Small Luxury Hotels of the World.

Windermere · Country house

Holbeck Ghyll

Former hunting lodge above Windermere with views across to the Langdale Pikes. Oak-panelled rooms, two AA Rosettes, a serious wine list.

Ambleside · Country house

The Samling

Above Windermere, set on 15 acres. The rooms are grand and the driveway curves up through the grounds.

Grasmere · Country house

Forest Side

Victorian gothic house on the edge of Grasmere. Paul Leonard's kitchen, one Michelin star and the Green Star.

Michelin

Borrowdale · Country house

Lodore Falls

On the southern shore of Derwentwater, with the falls rising directly behind the hotel. Spa, lake-facing rooms, and the Borrowdale valley on the doorstep.

Whinlatter Forest · Restaurant with rooms

The Cottage in the Wood

Inside the Whinlatter Forest. 9 bedrooms upstairs, a one-Michelin-star kitchen below. Jack and Beth Bond took the reins in 2024.

Michelin

Penrith · Country house

Askham Hall

Lowther estate, one Michelin star at Allium, Diane Butcher's kitchen gardens on the doorstep.

Michelin

Bassenthwaite Lake · Country house

Armathwaite Hall

Seventeenth-century manor on Bassenthwaite Lake, 400 acres, full spa, stables. Holds larger parties without feeling processed.

Tell us when you're travelling and what you'd like the week to feel like.

Plan a stay

The food

Kitchens of the region

Cumbria's kitchens cook from what's around them. L'Enclume's farm sits inside an hour's walk of the kitchen. Henrock cooks from Our Farm in the Cartmel Valley, a few miles down the road. Allium at Askham Hall takes from the estate's garden. The supply chain ends at the kitchen door.

For a private week or a single evening, the region offers more than a room. We arrange the right kitchens for your stay, property by property, from a table for two to a dinner for the family.

L'Enclume Cartmel
Three Michelin stars · Simon Rogan's flagship
Henrock at Linthwaite House Windermere
One Michelin star · Simon Rogan
Forest Side Grasmere
One Michelin star · country-house dining
Old Stamp House Ambleside
One Michelin star · Cumbrian tasting menu
Heft High Newton
One Michelin star · village inn, regional produce
Allium at Askham Hall Penrith
One Michelin star · estate kitchen and garden
SOURCE at The Gilpin Hotel Windermere
One Michelin star · sustainable tasting menu
The Cottage in the Wood Whinlatter Forest
One Michelin star · Jack and Beth Bond
Plan a Cumbria stay

From the founder

Why I send people to Cumbria

A morning on Loughrigg Fell with our hiking guide. He didn't push the climb; he took us on a quieter route most people miss. At the top, a private picnic of Herdwick lamb charcuterie and local cheeses was already laid out. The people running the operations actually know the place, the food on the plate comes from inside walking distance, and the day fits the guests in front of them.

I send people to Cumbria for that. It's quieter than the Cotswolds, more private than the Scottish highlands. The kitchens cook from what's around them. The houses run on first-name relationships.

Myles Longfield

Founder, ProLuxe Travel

How we work
